AgentCertificateBundle.description:
One-time deployment material returned after staging a generation.

The leaf private key is generated by Form and exists only in this return
value.  Neither the identity repository nor the CA service persists it.
``repr=False`` prevents accidental log disclosure while preserving explicit
serialization for the SFTP deployment boundary.

How a detection runs — surfaced explicitly so admin can choose up front.

daemon that keeps detecting and streaming events (``resident``).

schema.title: Response List Agent Identities Agent Identities Get

post.description: Discard a failed staged deployment without touching the old active cert.
Commit a successfully installed generation; old active overlaps briefly.
Immediately revoke one generation or the entire endpoint identity.
Stage a replacement certificate without invalidating the active one.

get.description: List the durable access credentials registered targets reference.
Fetch a single credential's status (mode, fingerprint, targets using it).

post.description:
Revoke a managed key: remove it from the target's authorized_keys and delete
the local key files. Targets that referenced it become unscannable until
re-registered with a fresh bootstrap.
Probe whether the credential can still authenticate to its target.

get.description: List durable job heads, newest creation first.

post.description: Durably enqueue a scan; ``Idempotency-Key`` makes client retries safe.

get.description: Fetch a scan job (its latest state) by id.

post.description: Cancel queued work immediately or request cooperative active cleanup.
Explicitly reset one failed/cancelled durable job and requeue it.

get.description: List registered targets, newest registration per target_id first.

post.description:
Register a scan target. A one-time `password` (managed_key mode, SSH) bootstraps
a managed key on the Form host and is then discarded — never persisted.

A ``transport=local`` target represents Form's own host (scan in place,
no SSH): it needs no credentials, so a password is rejected and any SSH credential
fields are normalized away (credential_mode=none, identity_path=None).

get.description: Fetch a single registered target by id.

post.description: Issue one staged bundle; its private key is returned exactly once.

get.description:
Probe whether the resident guard daemon is alive on a target (常驻 status).

Unreachable/auth failures degrade to ``alive=false`` rather than erroring, so a
polling UI shows an honest "unknown/unreachable" instead of a hard failure.

post.description:
Revoke Guard leaf certificates, then best-effort stop the remote daemon.

The durable target-operation lease serializes this direct API mutation with
scan workers in every Form process. Certificate revocation deliberately
precedes SSH teardown: an unreachable or immediately respawned daemon must
not retain a valid mTLS identity.
